note: Node view permissions has no config form, no services, and no submodules — its whole surface is a pair of dynamically-declared per-content-type permissions enforced through the node access grant system. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source (the .module + permissions class) to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the node_view_permissions module, what two permissions does it add for each content type, and what are their machine-name patterns (say for a content type with id `article`)?
evals.expected_output: For each type it adds 'view any {type} content' and 'view own {type} content' — e.g. 'view any article content' and 'view own article content'.

evals.prompt: Why would I install node_view_permissions instead of relying on Drupal core? What per-content-type access does core provide and what does it lack?
evals.expected_output: Core has per-type create/edit own/any/delete own/any but no per-type VIEW permission (only the broad 'access content'); this module adds per-type 'view own/any content' to fill that gap.

evals.prompt: If I grant a role 'view own article content' with node_view_permissions, which article nodes can those users see, and does it work for anonymous users?
evals.expected_output: Only articles owned by the current user (grant scoped to the node's owner id); it requires an authenticated account, so anonymous users never match a view_own realm.

evals.prompt: How does node_view_permissions enforce its view permissions under the hood — which Drupal node access hooks does it implement, and does that mean it also filters Views and search?
evals.expected_output: It uses the node access grant system: hook_node_access_records() writes per-node grant records and hook_node_grants() returns the realms an account qualifies for. Because it's grant-based (not hook_node_access), it also filters Views, listings, and search.

evals.prompt: With node_view_permissions, what does a user need in order to view UNPUBLISHED nodes of a given content type?
evals.expected_output: Both the per-type permission (view any/own {type} content) AND core's 'view any/own unpublished content' permission; the module only adds the unpublished realms when both are held.

evals.prompt: Where do I configure node_view_permissions — is there an admin settings form, and what is the configure route?
evals.expected_output: There is no settings form and no configure route; you manage the permissions on the standard People → Permissions page (/admin/people/permissions).

evals.prompt: What does node_view_permissions do on install regarding existing roles and node access, so the site keeps behaving as before?
evals.expected_output: On install it flags a node access rebuild and grants 'view any {type} content' to the anonymous and authenticated roles for every existing content type, preserving core's default published-content visibility.

evals.prompt: How are node_view_permissions' permissions declared given that a site can have any number of content types — static YAML or dynamic, and via what class?
evals.expected_output: Dynamically, via a permission_callbacks entry in node_view_permissions.permissions.yml pointing at NodeViewPermissionsPermissions (which extends core NodePermissions and overrides buildPermissions), so permissions are generated per content type automatically.
